skill.name: repo-intake-and-plan
skill.description: Scans AI repositories for README evidence, common project files, and documented reproduction commands. Returns conservative classifications and a minimal reproduction plan without running commands.
skill.summary: Scans AI repositories and recommends the smallest trustworthy reproduction path.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography and command execution are false positives after context review. The skill includes helper scripts that read local repository files and print resolved paths, so publication is acceptable with a low filesystem warning.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Inspection And Path Disclosure
low_findings.description: The helper scripts read a user-supplied repository path and README file. Output includes resolved local paths, so users should avoid scanning sensitive directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file reads and resolved path output are visible in the scripts. The behavior is limited to local user-selected paths.
low_findings.recommendation: Document that users should run the skill only on repositories they intend to inspect.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick References
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as shell execution. These lines list filenames, directories, and command categories in documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside Markdown documentation. It names files and categories, with no interpreter context or executable code path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Command Extraction Is Not Execution
low_findings.description: The extractor parses README code fences and recognizes shell-like text, including PowerShell blocks. It records commands as strings and never invokes a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code uses regular expressions, filtering, dictionaries, and JSON output. No subprocess, os.system, eval, or shell invocation appears.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep execution boundaries documented so users know extracted commands are not run.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged descriptive text and argparse descriptions as weak cryptography. No hashing, encryption, digest API, or credential handling appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations contain prose or command-line parser help text. No cryptographic operation is present in the reviewed context.
low_findings.recommendation: No security remediation is needed for these flags.

content.value_statement: AI research repositories often hide the safest reproduction path among many scripts and setup notes. This skill scans documented evidence and returns a conservative plan for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Scans README and common project files for reproduction signals.
Extracts shell-like commands from README code blocks and inline prompts.
Classifies commands as inference, evaluation, training, setup, asset, or other.
Summarizes top-level repository structure and high-signal directories.
Recommends the smallest trustworthy reproduction target from documented evidence.
Records ambiguity when a command or entry point is only inferred.
limitations: Does not install packages, download assets, or run reproduction commands.
Does not verify that extracted commands work in the current environment.
Depends on available README and project files for reliable recommendations.
May miss undocumented workflows hidden in source code.

use_cases.title: Triage A New Research Repository
use_cases.description: Find documented setup, inference, evaluation, and training paths before running anything.

use_cases.title: Prepare A Reproduction Plan
use_cases.description: Convert README evidence into a minimal plan that an orchestrator can review later.

use_cases.title: Compare Candidate Entry Points
use_cases.description: Classify commands by purpose so a reviewer can select a low-risk starting point.

prompt_templates.title: Scan The Repository
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner intake for a local repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan this repository and summarize the README, common project files, documented commands, and the smallest trustworthy reproduction target.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Inference First
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose a lightweight path before evaluation or training.
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the repository with an inference-first priority. Separate setup, assets, inference, evaluation, and training commands, and recommend the safest next step.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Reproduction Evidence
prompt_templates.scenario: Check whether a plan is supported by documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the repository evidence for the proposed reproduction path. Mark each command as documented or inferred, and list ambiguity that needs confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Build An Orchestrator Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handoff to another agent or workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise handoff with repository structure, command inventory, category decisions, risks, missing assets, and the minimum trustworthy reproduction recommendation.

output_examples.input: Scan a vision model repository with a README and example scripts.
output: Repository summary lists README, requirements file, scripts directory, and configs directory.
Command inventory separates installation, checkpoint download, demo inference, and evaluation commands.
Recommendation selects the documented demo command before training or full benchmark reproduction.

output_examples.input: Review a repository with several training scripts but weak documentation.
output: The plan marks training commands as inferred when they come from filenames only.
Ambiguity notes identify missing dataset instructions and checkpoint requirements.
The suggested next step is README-supported setup plus a non-training smoke check.

best_practices: Provide the repository path and any user priority, such as inference-first.
Review extracted commands before executing them in a separate workflow.
Use documented README evidence before relying on filename-based guesses.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to execute commands or install environments.
Do not treat inferred command classifications as verified reproduction proof.
Do not scan private directories that are unrelated to the target repository.

faq.question: Does this skill run repository commands?
faq.answer: No. It extracts and classifies documented commands, but it does not execute them.

faq.question: Which files does it inspect first?
faq.answer: It prioritizes README files, dependency files, setup files, Dockerfiles, and common configuration directories.

faq.question: Can it choose between inference and training?
faq.answer: Yes. It classifies candidates and usually recommends the smallest documented path first.

faq.question: Does it download checkpoints or datasets?
faq.answer: No. It may identify asset commands, but downloads belong in a separate execution workflow.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is structured for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What should I review before running any command?
faq.answer: Review command source, category, required assets, environment assumptions, and any ambiguity reported by the skill.
